chunks.text: AISES: Great Scholarship Opportunity with Intel! Applications Closing May 15 - EECS at Berkeley. I hope you can help me get the word out about this scholarship for Native students – both undergraduates and graduates. I am extending the scholarship deadline for the Intel scholarships to May 15, 2018. Thank you so much for your help in getting the word out about this scholarship.
chunks.question: What is the extended deadline for the Intel scholarship applications?

chunks.text: The Intel scholarships are $5,000 for undergraduate and $10,000 for graduate students and they have a summer internship component. Please see the attached flyer and distribute far and wide to Native American/Alaska Native, Native Hawaiian, Pacific Islander students.
chunks.question: What are the scholarship amounts for undergraduate and graduate students?

chunks.text: To qualify, students must: Be studying computer science, material science, or computer, electrical, or chemical engineering (as an intended major for undergraduate freshmen, pursuing these degrees as an enrolled undergraduate or graduate student). Have a 3.0 GPA (in last year of high school or during the last semester completed). Be an AISES member. Be interested in an internship for the summer of 2018.
chunks.question: What are the academic requirements for the scholarship?
chunks.answer: Study specific fields and have a 3.0 GPA.

chunks.text: An enrolled member or a decedent of an enrolled member of a federal or state recognized American Indian Tribe or Alaska Native Village; or Native Hawaiian or decedent from a Native Hawaiian; or Pacific Islander or decedent from Pacific Islander; or Indigenous person of Canada. Enrollment documents and/or a copy of birth certificate(s) showing descendancy from an enrolled citizen, Indigenous group of Canada, Native Hawaiian, or Pacific Islander are acceptable. Applicant may also, instead, provide a copy of their Certificate of Degree of Indian Blood or Certificate of Degree of Alaska Native Blood (CDIB) card. Please note, AISES may not always have scholarships available for non-citizens of the United States.
chunks.question: What eligibility criteria apply based on heritage?
chunks.answer: Must be Native American, Alaska Native, Native Hawaiian, Pacific Islander, or Indigenous Canadian.

chunks.question: Where can students apply for the scholarship?
